ops.title: UV_EXCLUDE_NEWER=1h in this env hides just-published packages from uvx; override when a release must be consumed immediately
ops.body:
This environment sets `UV_EXCLUDE_NEWER=1h` (a rolling 1-hour cutoff). `uvx <tool>` resolves an **ephemeral** env subject to it, so a package published in the last hour is invisible — `uvx` silently picks the previous version instead.

**Symptom:** publish capt-hook 3.6.0, then `uvx capt-hook ...` runs 3.5.0 (older, missing the new feature) with a confusing downstream error (e.g. "pack manifest capt-hook.toml missing").

**Fix:** run the consumer with the cutoff cleared — `env -u UV_EXCLUDE_NEWER <cmd>`. This is required whenever a release must be used in the same session it was published (cc-notes rollout's `cc-notes init` → `uvx capt-hook pack add`, capt-hook PyPI verifies, etc.). Note `uvx` ignores a `uv tool install`ed version for ephemeral resolution, so upgrading the installed tool alone is not enough — clear the cutoff.

**Also:** the global `capt-hook` uv tool was stuck at the ancient v0.11.1 (pre-`pack`); upgraded to 3.6.0 via `uv tool install capt-hook@3.6.0 --force`. Related: [[rtk-git-staging-unreliable]] (git staging caveat that also bit the rollout's commit loop).

**Deeper trap (2026-07-02, capt-hook 5.0.0 pack migration):** clearing the env var is NOT always enough. A resolution done under `UV_EXCLUDE_NEWER` bakes a stale version into uv's **preference cache** (verbose shows `Selecting: <pkg>==<oldver> [preference]`). After that, `env -u UV_EXCLUDE_NEWER uv run ...` STILL prefers the old version — the preference outlives the env var. Also, a cached **PEP 723 script env** (`~/.cache/uv/environments-v2/<hash>/`) and the cached **simple index** both pin the stale resolve. To force the fresh version: `--upgrade-package <pkg>` (ignores the preference), or `--no-cache` / `--refresh` (re-fetch index + re-resolve). A full `uv cache clean` fixes it permanently but needs the exclusive cache lock, which resident `uvx --from semble[mcp]` MCP-server processes hold — so it hangs; don't wait on it, use the per-invocation flags. This bit the bare `uv run --with 'capt-hook==5.0.0' plugin/hooks/tests/test_cc_notes.py` (cc-transcript pinned to 7.0.1, missing `WorkflowCall`); see [[capt-hook-5-cc-transcript-pin-too-loose]].
